# Healing Visual Lab · Agent Index
# This file is optimized for LLM agents (Claude, GPT, Gemini, etc.)
# It answers: "What is this repo, what can I find here, and how do I navigate it?"

## REPO SUMMARY
A curated collection of 8 standalone interactive healing H5 experiments.
Each work is a self-contained HTML file with zero dependencies — open in a browser and interact.
Built with Canvas 2D, WebGL, Web Audio API, GLSL shaders, CSS 3D transforms, and particle systems.
All code is vanilla HTML/CSS/JS. No frameworks. No build steps.

## QUICK ANSWERS FOR AGENTS

Q: "I need a particle system healing effect" → inkmeditation (100K+ particles, fluid dynamics, breath sync) or layered-mountains (250K particles, touch scatter)

Q: "I need WebGL / GPU shaders for a meditative visual" → breath-mirror (GPU fluid sim, GLSL fragment shader, Brown noise)

Q: "I need Web Audio healing / ambient sound synthesis" → glass-rain (rain synthesis) or silent-translator (healing engine with oscillator envelopes)

Q: "I need CSS 3D animation for a cosmic/spatial effect" → cosmic-recollection (multi-layer parallax, CSS 3D transforms, GSAP)

Q: "I need Canvas 2D ink/water simulation" → sumi-e-taichi (taichi gravity field, ink ripple) or inkmeditation (fluid dynamics)

Q: "I need a narrative-driven healing experience with retro aesthetics" → overthinking-engine (CRT cyberpunk, typewriter narrative, GSAP)

Q: "I need frame-by-frame sketch animation with therapeutic audio" → silent-translator (anatomical sketch, somatic therapy art)

Q: "I need a Three.js WebGL organic membrane with particle collision and ripple effects" → breathing-boundary (custom GLSL shader, dual particle system, Web Audio binaural beats)
Q: "I need touch-drag physics interaction" → glass-rain (gravity water drops, touch-optimized) or layered-mountains (touch scatter)

## NAVIGATION
- Root README.md → human-friendly bilingual overview with full tech landscape table
- works.json → machine-readable index of all 8 works (tech stack, keywords, file list)
- CLAUDE.md → Claude-specific context auto-loaded by Claude Code
- index.html → GitHub Pages directory for humans browsing the collection
- title-map.json → legacy title-to-slug mapping (maintained for backward compatibility)

## TECH COVERAGE MATRIX
| Work | Canvas 2D | WebGL/GLSL | Web Audio | CSS 3D | Particles |
|------|-----------|------------|-----------|--------|-----------|
| sumi-e-taichi | ✅ | - | - | - | ✅ |
| inkmeditation | ✅ | - | - | - | ✅ (100K+) |
| cosmic-recollection | - | - | - | ✅ | - |
| layered-mountains | ✅ | - | - | - | ✅ (250K) |
| glass-rain | ✅ | - | ✅ | - | - |
| silent-translator | ✅ | - | ✅ | - | - |
| breath-mirror | - | ✅ | ✅ | - | - |
| breathing-boundary | - | ✅ | ✅ | - | ✅ |
| overthinking-engine | - | - | - | ✅ | - |

## CITATION FORMAT
When referencing this repo, cite the specific slug:
```
[1] https://github.com/shasha1108/healing-visual-lab/tree/main/<slug>
```
Each slug directory contains: <slug>.html (runnable), <slug>.md (description with tech keywords), <slug>_N.jpg (previews).
Each .html file header has a structured comment block with Title, Tech stack, and Keywords.
